edges.description: Altered root sheath morphogenesis produces enlarged pulp chambers.
edges.source: Failed invagination of Hertwig epithelial root sheath diaphragm

edges.description: Furcation failure yields classic taurodont root-trunk morphology.
Dysregulated HERS translational control contributes to defective root development.
KCTD1 variant-associated developmental dysregulation can co-occur with reduced tooth number.

nodes.description: Taurodontism is characterized by an enlarged pulp cavity/chamber.

evidence.reference_title: Prevalence of taurodontism: meta-analysis in recent humans and evolutionary perspectives.
evidence.snippet: Taurodontism is a continuous anatomical variation of permanent and primary posterior teeth represented by an enlargement of the pulp cavity.

nodes.description: Root furcation is displaced apically with altered root-trunk configuration.

evidence.reference_title: Epithelial Wnt10a Is Essential for Tooth Root Furcation Morphogenesis.
evidence.snippet: Wnt10a knockout in the whole tissue (EIIa-Cre;Wnt10aflox/flox) and in dental epithelium (K14-Cre;Wnt10aflox/flox) led to an absence of or apically located root furcation in molars of mice, a phenotype that resembled taurodontism.

nodes.description: Axin1-dependent control of AKT1-mTORC1 signaling in Hertwig epithelial root sheath regulates selective Shh translation and root morphogenesis; dysregulation produces defective roots.

evidence.snippet: Further studies reveal that Axin1 in the HERS negatively regulates the AKT1-mTORC1 pathway through binding to AKT1, leading to inhibition of ribosomal biogenesis and mRNA translation.
Sonic hedgehog (Shh) protein, a morphogen essential for root development, is over-synthesized by upregulated mTORC1 activity upon Axin1 inactivation.

nodes.description: Loss of epithelial WNT10A signaling disrupts furcation formation and drives taurodont-like root morphology, implicating epithelial-mesenchymal signaling defects in root development.

evidence.reference_title: Epithelial Wnt10a Is Essential for Tooth Root Furcation Morphogenesis.
evidence.snippet: our study provides the first in vivo evidence that epithelial Wnt10a guides root furcation formation and plays a crucial role in controlling the organized proliferation of adjacent mesenchymal cells by regulating proper Wnt4 expression during root furcation morphogenesis.

nodes.description: Taurodont morphology is linked to altered root-sheath invagination, shifting furcation apically and expanding the pulp chamber in multirooted teeth.
nodes.id: Failed invagination of Hertwig epithelial root sheath diaphragm

evidence.reference_title: Taurodontism: a review of the condition and endodontic treatment challenges.
evidence.snippet: Taurodontism can be defined as a change in tooth shape caused by the failure of Hertwig's epithelial sheath diaphragm to invaginate at the proper horizontal level.

nodes.description: Pathogenic KCTD1 variants segregate with taurodontism and related root anomalies, implicating altered WNT-SHH-BMP signaling control in human dental morphogenesis.

evidence.snippet: The variants segregated with the dental anomalies in all nine patients from the two families.
Functional studies on the p.Arg241Gln variant are consistent with an impact on β-catenin levels and canonical WNT signaling.

nodes.description: Defining radiographic phenotype with enlarged pulp chambers and apical displacement of pulpal floor/furcation in multirooted teeth.

evidence.reference_title: Taurodontism: a review of the condition and endodontic treatment challenges.
evidence.snippet: An enlarged pulp chamber, apical displacement of the pulpal floor, and no constriction at the level of the cementoenamel junction are the characteristic features.

nodes.description: Reduced tooth number is an associated phenotype in KCTD1-associated dental anomaly presentations including taurodontism.

evidence.snippet: Clinical findings of the patients included taurodontism, unseparated roots, long roots, tooth agenesis, a supernumerary tooth, torus palatinus, and torus mandibularis.
